message:
fix(runtime): use atomic CAS for blocking pool idle-thread retirement to prevent undershoot

The blocking pool's idle retirement logic had a TOCTOU race: a worker
checked active_threads > min_threads, then later decremented
active_threads in the spawning thread's join path. Between the check
and the decrement, multiple workers could independently decide to retire,
dropping the thread count below min_threads.

Introduced try_claim_idle_retirement() which uses compare_exchange_weak
in a loop to atomically claim exactly one retirement slot per floor
level. The decrement happens inside the CAS, so only one worker succeeds
when active_threads == min_threads + 1.

Changed blocking_worker_loop() to return a bool indicating whether the
worker retired with a claimed slot. The spawning thread now only
decrements active_threads if the worker did NOT claim a retirement slot
(i.e., exited for other reasons like shutdown), preventing double-
decrement.

Added test: two threads race to retire from a pool with min_threads=1,
active_threads=2. Exactly one claims the slot, and active_threads never
drops below min_threads.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
